element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Generates funding and determines alien technology advancement
Player performance influences alien weaponry advancement; difficulty self-adjusts
Must maintain rapport with 13+ organizations to sustain funding and prevent hostile actions
Exposed through specimen gathering missions; understanding dependencies between 14 alien races becomes critical
Weaponized alien that attacks individuals; hatches and jumps on heads after pod landing
Player must learn to send aircraft and agents through tetrahedron gates to alien homeworld
Final objective; reveals aliens have enslaved Sectoid survivors from previous wars
Can steal items from corporations to maintain X-COM operations if government support withdrawn
Aliens attempt to infest and control organizations; can spread across Mega-Primus
Triggered by alien interference or excessive property damage; can create financial stress requiring alternative income

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dual-cycle rhythm: management-layer pacing (weekly performance cycles, organizational pressure) overlaid with mission-based tactical pacing (UFO interceptions, battlescape encounters). Self-adjusting difficulty creates meta-rhythm where player success accelerates subsequent challenge.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Raid missions are player-initiated optional battlescape encounters; not mandatory for progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to dual-mode gameplay (Cityscape management + Battlescape tactics); initial UFO interceptions and basic alien encounters; learning faction diplomacy
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased alien complexity (14 races introduced); organism life cycle mechanics become relevant; financial pressure escalates; optional raids available as alternative income; Brain-Sucker threat emergence
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dimensional gate infiltration introduced; preparation for alien homeworld invasion; potential military dominance of Mega-Primus; endgame alien threat resolution
notable_spikes: First major UFO interception success required to establish funding credibility
Brain-Sucker specimen gathering missions (infection threat introduces new survival mechanic)
Government funding loss scenario (forces player to shift strategy toward raids or organizational manipulation)
Dimensional gate encounters (new combat environment and mechanics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Controlled alien capture missions where player must survive alien attacks (particularly Brain-Suckers) while securing specimens. Reveals alien life cycle dependencies and informs research priorities.
Aerial combat phase determines alien equipment quality in future battlescape missions. Success/failure creates positive feedback loop affecting difficulty scaling.
Optional battlescape encounter initiated by player to secure resources and maintain funding when organizational support threatened. High-risk but strategically necessary.
Late-game battlescape missions set in extradimensional alien territory. Reveals enslaved Sectoids and establishes narrative connection to previous X-COM games.
